briefing.incidentSummary:
₹85,000 was transferred from a senior citizen's bank account to an unknown account at 3:14 AM.

The victim never initiated the transfer. Their phone was found at home.

Someone used a cloned SIM card to intercept the OTP.
briefing.missionText:
Your Mission:

› Trace the SIM cloning operation

› Identify who intercepted the OTP

› Collect at least 3 correct evidences

› Flag the right suspect

suspects.name: Vikram B
suspects.profileNotes: Accessed victim's SIM provisioning record at 3:05 AM — 9 minutes before the fraudulent transfer. Issued a duplicate SIM request without a physical store visit.

suspects.name: Unknown Caller
suspects.profileNotes: Called victim the previous day posing as a bank employee. Collected account number but NOT OTP — the OTP was intercepted separately via SIM clone.

suspects.name: Rahul V
suspects.profileNotes: Visited the evening before. No technical knowledge of SIM provisioning. Alibi confirmed for 3 AM window.

suspects.name: Deepa S
suspects.profileNotes: Store was closed at 3 AM. Her credentials were not used in the SIM duplication — the backend system was accessed directly.

items.label: OTP received on cloned SIM: 847291
items.detail: Internal message from Vikram B to an unknown number at 3:13 AM — one minute before the transfer. He received and forwarded the OTP directly.

items.label: Transfer done. Delete this.
items.detail: Sent at 3:16 AM — 2 minutes after the fraudulent transfer completed. Confirms Vikram knew about and participated in the fraud.

items.label: Sir your account will be blocked. Please confirm your account number.
items.detail: Phishing call transcript from the day before. Collected account number but not the critical OTP — the OTP interception happened separately via SIM clone.

items.label: sim_provisioning_log.txt
items.detail: Backend log shows duplicate SIM issued for victim's number at 3:07 AM. Request initiated by employee ID VB-4421 — Vikram B — without any physical ID verification.

items.label: bank_transfer_record.pdf
items.detail: ₹85,000 transferred at 3:14 AM to account ACC-9921-X. OTP authenticated at 3:13 AM from a different device than victim's registered phone.

items.label: employee_access_log.txt
items.detail: Vikram B accessed the SIM provisioning portal at 3:05 AM — outside of his assigned shift hours (9 AM–6 PM). Unauthorized off-hours access.

hiddenItem.label: destination_account_trace.txt
hiddenItem.detail: Account ACC-9921-X traced to a mule account opened with Vikram B's Aadhaar number under a false name. Direct financial link to the perpetrator.

minigame.title: Decode Account Reference
minigame.hint: Caesar shift -3. Whose name is linked to the mule account?
hints: It is a person's name followed by their initial.

onSuccess.message: destination_account_trace.txt is now available in your file feed.

items.label: OTP Authentication Device
items.detail: OTP was authenticated from device IMEI 867530041234567 — not the victim's registered phone (IMEI 356789012345678). Confirms a different device received the OTP.

items.label: Backend Access IP
items.detail: IP 27.56.88.201 accessed the SIM provisioning backend at 3:05 AM. This IP is assigned to a workstation at SimConnect's night shift office — traced to Vikram B's desk.

timeline.title: Phishing Call
timeline.description: Victim receives fake bank call, account number collected

timeline.title: Backend Access
timeline.description: Vikram B logs into SIM provisioning system off-hours

timeline.title: SIM Cloned
timeline.description: Duplicate SIM issued for victim's number

timeline.title: OTP Intercepted
timeline.description: Bank OTP received on cloned SIM by Vikram B

perfect.title: Case Solved!
perfect.subtitle: Vikram B exposed as the SIM fraud perpetrator.
perfect.label: CASE CLOSED

wrongAccusation.title: Wrong Suspect
wrongAccusation.subtitle: The real culprit was Vikram B.
wrongAccusation.label: CASE FAILED

aria.welcomeToHub:
...₹85,000. Gone. At 3 in the morning.
The victim was asleep. Their phone never moved.
Someone intercepted the signal itself.
aria.exploreFeeds:
SIM logs. Bank records. OTP device traces.
The fraud was surgical. Find the surgeon.
aria.viewEvidence:
Off-hours access. Unauthorized SIM duplication.
This wasn't random.
aria.decryptionHint:
The mule account has a name behind it.
Decode the reference.
aria.flagSuspect:
A trace is forming.
You're on to something — keep pulling the thread.
More fragments remain. Don't stop now.
